mnemonics: FIRP: Focus on your own payoff
keyPoints: Finitely Repeated Prisoner's Dilemma (FIRP): Both players choose actions independently and receive payoffs based on their combined actions.
Infinitely Repeated Prisoner's Dilemma (IRP): Both players observe each other's actions in each period and make decisions based on their expectations of future actions.
Nash Equilibrium: In FIRP, both players choose to defect (D) in every period, resulting in a payoff of (10, 10).
Trembling Hand Persuasion: In IRP, players can change their strategy over time to improve their payoff.
Focal Point Theorem: Players tend to converge to the same strategy in IRP, regardless of their initial strategy.
